title: SQL Injection
description: Attacker can inject SQL statements through user input to manipulate database queries
remediation_steps: Use parameterized queries or prepared statements
Apply input validation and output encoding

title: Cross-Site Scripting (XSS)
description: Attacker can inject client-side scripts into web pages viewed by other users
remediation_steps: Implement output encoding for all user-supplied data
Set strict Content Security Policy headers

title: Remote Code Execution
description: Attacker can execute arbitrary code on the target server

title: Server-Side Request Forgery
description: Attacker can make the server send requests to unintended locations
remediation_steps: Validate and restrict URL inputs to allowlisted domains
Block requests to internal network ranges

title: Local File Inclusion
description: Attacker can read arbitrary files from the server filesystem
remediation_steps: Validate and sanitize all file path inputs
Use allowlist of permitted file paths
Run application with minimal filesystem permissions

title: Weak TLS Configuration
description: Server supports outdated TLS versions or weak cipher suites
remediation_steps: Use TLS 1.2+ with strong cipher suites
Regularly test with tools like testssl.sh

title: Expired SSL Certificate
description: The server's SSL/TLS certificate has expired
remediation_steps: Set up automated certificate renewal (Let's Encrypt)

title: Missing SPF Record
description: Domain lacks SPF record allowing email spoofing

title: Missing DMARC Record
description: Domain lacks DMARC record for email authentication
remediation_steps: Publish a DMARC TXT record with p=reject

title: Unnecessary Open Port
description: Service exposed on a port that should not be publicly accessible

title: Exposed Secret/Credential
description: Sensitive credential found in source code, config, or public repository
remediation_steps: Revoke and rotate the exposed credential immediately
Audit all systems accessed with the compromised secret

title: Subdomain Takeover
description: Dangling DNS record pointing to unclaimed resource
remediation_steps: Claim or remove the dangling resource
Decommission resources before removing DNS entries

title: Cloud Misconfiguration
description: Cloud resource configured with excessive permissions or public access
remediation_steps: Disable public access on storage buckets

title: Container Image Vulnerability
description: Known vulnerability in a container image dependency
remediation_steps: Update the base image to latest patched version

title: Source Code Vulnerability
description: Security issue identified through static analysis of source code

title: CI/CD Pipeline Vulnerability
description: Security weakness in the CI/CD pipeline configuration
owasp_category: A08:2021 – Software and Data Integrity Failures

title: Active Directory Misconfiguration
description: Insecure AD configuration that could enable privilege escalation

title: DNS Misconfiguration
description: DNS configuration allowing zone transfer or information disclosure
remediation_steps: Disable zone transfers to unauthorized servers
Review and clean up stale records

title: WAF Bypass Possible
description: Web Application Firewall can be bypassed to reach the origin server
remediation_steps: Restrict origin server access to WAF IPs only
Implement mutual TLS between WAF and origin

title: Generic Vulnerability
description: A security vulnerability detected by automated scanning
remediation_steps: Implement compensating controls if no patch available

title: Information Exposure
description: Sensitive information is accessible to unauthorized parties

title: Security Misconfiguration
description: System or application configured insecurely
remediation_steps: Review against vendor security guidelines and CIS benchmarks

title: Injection Vulnerability
description: Untrusted data sent to an interpreter as part of a command or query
remediation_steps: Implement least privilege for downstream systems

title: Resource Takeover
description: Dangling resource that can be claimed by an attacker
remediation_steps: Claim or remove the dangling resource immediately
Audit DNS records and cloud resources regularly
Decommission resources before removing DNS entries
